perturbation.original_text: 2. After ROCHESTER receives a fully signed copy of this Agreement, ROCHESTER shall disclose to COMPANY Confidential Information solely for use by COMPANY in its internal evaluation of the Confidential Information’s commercial prospects.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. After ROCHESTER receives a fully signed copy of this Agreement, ROCHESTER shall disclose to COMPANY Protected Information solely for use by COMPANY in its internal evaluation of the Protected Information’s commercial prospects.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" is replaced with "Protected Information" in Section 2, but the definition in Section 1 still refers to “Proprietary Data” and the rest of the agreement uses "Confidential Information", leading to confusion about which term applies. If "Protected Information" is not equivalent to "Proprietary Data" as defined in section 1 or “Confidential Information” as used in the rest of the contract, Rochester's obligation becomes unclear and creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. COMPANY agree that, for a period of five (5) ye ars after the date of its receipt of the last Confidential Information disclosed under the Agreement, it shall: (i) keep Confidential Information confidential; and (ii) not use the Confidential Information for any commercial purpose. The foregoing shall not apply to that part of any Confidential Information that: (a) is disclosed or used by COMPANY in a ccordance with any written consent granted by ROCHESTER; or (b) at the time of receipt by COMPANY wa s independently know n by COMPANY; or (c) at any time becomes generally known to the public through no fault of COMPANY; or (d) has been or is made available to COMPANY by a third party having the lawful right to do so without breaching any obligation of nonuse or confidentiality to ROCHESTER; or (e) has been or is disclosed to others by ROCHESTER without similar restrictions on disclosure and use; or (f) COMPANY is required to disclose pursuant to an order of a judicial or administrative authority
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Confidential Information", and the contradicted text explicitly defines obligations related to "Confidential Information". The changed text replaces "Confidential Information" with "Protected Information" in Section 2. As the explanation states, if "Protected Information" is not equivalent to “Confidential Information” or "Proprietary Data”, it is unclear what Rochester is obligated to disclose, leading to confusion about whether section 3's terms apply and creating a direct conflict. The definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction states that a contradiction exists when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3. COMPANY agree that, for a period of five (5) ye ars after the date of its receipt of the last Confidential Information disclosed under the Agreement, it shall: (i) keep Confidential Information confidential; and (ii) not use the Confidential Information for any commercial purpose. The foregoing shall not apply to that part of any Confidential Information that: (a) is disclosed or used by COMPANY in a ccordance with any written consent granted by ROCHESTER; or (b) at the time of receipt by COMPANY wa s independently know n by COMPANY; or (c) at any time becomes generally known to the public through no fault of COMPANY; or (d) has been or is made available to COMPANY by a third party having the lawful right to do so without breaching any obligation of nonuse or confidentiality to ROCHESTER; or (e) has been or is disclosed to others by ROCHESTER without similar restrictions on disclosure and use; or (f) COMPANY is required to disclose pursuant to an order of a judicial or administrative authority
perturbation.changed_text: 3. COMPANY agree that, for a period of five (5) ye ars after the date of its receipt of the last Private Data disclosed under the Agreement, it shall: (i) keep Private Data confidential; and (ii) not use the Private Data for any commercial purpose. The foregoing shall not apply to that part of any Private Data that: (a) is disclosed or used by COMPANY in a ccordance with any written consent granted by ROCHESTER; or (b) at the time of receipt by COMPANY wa s independently know n by COMPANY; or (c) at any time becomes generally known to the public through no fault of COMPANY; or (d) has been or is made available to COMPANY by a third party having the lawful right to do so without breaching any obligation of nonuse or confidentiality to ROCHESTER; or (e) has been or is disclosed to others by ROCHESTER without similar restrictions on disclosure and use; or (f) COMPANY is required to disclose pursuant to an order of a judicial or administrative authority
perturbation.explanation: In section 3, "Confidential Information" is replaced with "Private Data". Given the earlier changes to "Proprietary Data" and “Protected Information” and the continuing use of "Confidential Information" elsewhere in the document, it becomes unclear which term, if any, COMPANY is actually obligated to protect. This introduces internal contradictions because section 2 refers to "Protected Information", Section 3 to "Private Data" while Section 1 contains the definition for "Proprietary Data", and the rest of the text uses the original "Confidential Information."
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. At the end of the Evaluation Period, COMPANY shall upon request of ROCHESTER, return to ROCHESTER all Confidential Information in its files to monitor its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data' in Section 3. The contradicted text, Section 6, refers to 'Confidential Information'. Because Section 3 now discusses 'Private Data' instead of 'Confidential Information', it creates a contradiction, as it becomes unclear whether COMPANY is obligated to return 'Confidential Information' per Section 6, since their obligations in Section 3 are now tied to 'Private Data'. This misalignment of terminology creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. COMPANY shall complete its evaluation within the period expiring on the date six (6) months after the date COMPANY receives the Confidential Information from ROCHESTER. If COMPANY believes the Confidential Information has scientific and commer cial promise, COMPANY and ROCHESTER may elect to commence negotiations l eading to an agreemen t governing COMPANY’s commercialization of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. COMPANY shall complete its assessment within the period expiring on the date six (6) months after the date COMPANY receives the Confidential Information from ROCHESTER. If COMPANY believes the Confidential Information has scientific and commer cial promise, COMPANY and ROCHESTER may elect to commence negotiations l eading to an agreemen t governing COMPANY’s commercialization of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The term “evaluation” is replaced with “assessment” in Section 5, which leads to a contradictory interpretation, because assessment can mean something different that evaluation. Given the rest of the document it's referring to an “evaluation”, making the use of “assessment” create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. At the end of the Evaluation Period, COMPANY shall upon request of ROCHESTER, return to ROCHESTER all Confidential Information in its files to monitor its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "evaluation" to "assessment" in Section 5 introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text in Section 6 refers specifically to the "Evaluation Period". By changing the term to "assessment", it creates ambiguity about whether the "Evaluation Period" in Section 6 still applies, leading to a potential in-text contradiction. The Misaligned Terminology definition applies.

perturbation.original_text: 6. At the end of the Evaluation Period, COMPANY shall upon request of ROCHESTER, return to ROCHESTER all Confidential Information in its files to monitor its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. At the end of the Assessment Term, COMPANY shall upon request of ROCHESTER, return to ROCHESTER all Confidential Information in its files to monitor its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term “Evaluation Period” is replaced with “Assessment Term” in Section 6, but the term Evaluation remains intact in the rest of the document, contradicting the document itself. “Assessment term” and “Evaluation” are not defined, and could mean different things which create contradiction within the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. COMPANY shall complete its assessment within the period expiring on the date six (6) months after the date COMPANY receives the Confidential Information from ROCHESTER. If COMPANY believes the Confidential Information has scientific and commer cial promise, COMPANY and ROCHESTER may elect to commence negotiations l eading to an agreemen t governing COMPANY’s commercialization of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that replacing “Evaluation Period” with “Assessment Term” in Section 6, while leaving "Evaluation" intact in the rest of the document, creates a contradiction. The contradicted text mentions a timeline for assessment, and using different terms for the same or similar process introduces ambiguity, creating a conflict within the document according to the Misaligned Terminology definition. Since "Assessment term" and "Evaluation" are not defined, and could mean different things which create contradiction within the document.
